source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 10. TCF_ADD(HAD1417MCW) 230215 (Rev.5) - Label Update/Attachment 10. Clinical Evaluation/Attachment 10-8 Vigilance System procedure/AQP808 Vigilance System form.xls

sourceSummary.oneLine: Template set for medical device vigilance reporting, including incident, FSCA, periodic summary, and trend reports to National Competent Authorities.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to collect and submit manufacturer vigilance information, Field Safety Corrective Action details, Periodic Summary Reports, and Trend Reports for medical devices distributed in EEA, Switzerland and Turkey.
keyPoints: Includes Manufacturer's Incident Report fields for incident classification, submitter, manufacturer, device, patient, healthcare facility, investigation, and distribution information.
Includes Field Safety Corrective Action Report fields for FSCA description, Field Safety Notice status, implementation schedule, and distribution countries.
Includes Periodic Summary Report fields for PSR type, failure mode or root cause basis, summary period, incident counts, and manufacturer investigation updates.
Includes Trend Report fields for identified trend, trend analysis period, trigger level, reportable events under vigilance, and corrective or preventive actions.

evidence.claim: The document contains a Manufacturer's Incident Report form.
The Manufacturer's Incident Report includes report types for initial, follow-up, combined initial and final, and final reports.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of report □ Initial report □ Follow-up report □ Combined Initial and final report □ Final report

evidence.claim: The Manufacturer's Incident Report asks whether an incident represents a serious public health threat.
evidence.evidenceText: Does the incident represent a serious public health threat? □ Yes □ No

evidence.claim: The Manufacturer's Incident Report classifies incidents as death, unanticipated serious deterioration in state of health, or all other reportable incident.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification of incident □ Death □ Unanticipated serious deterioration in state of health □ All other reportable incident

evidence.claim: The document contains a Field Safety Corrective Action Report form.
The FSCA report includes fields for background, reason, action description, user and distributor advice, FSCA progress, and implementation schedule.
evidence.evidenceText: Description of FSCA; Background information and reason for the FSCA; Description and justification of the action (corrective/preventive); Advice on actions to be taken by the distributor and the user; Progress of FSCA; Time schadule for the implementation of the different actions

evidence.claim: The FSCA report captures Field Safety Notice attachments and status.
evidence.evidenceText: Attached please find FSN Status □ Field Safety Notice(FSN) in English □ Draft FSN □ FSN in national launguage □ Final FSN

evidence.claim: The document contains a Periodic Summary Report form.
The Periodic Summary Report supports reporting incidents described in a Field Safety Notice or common and well documented incidents.
evidence.evidenceText: PSR Type : □ Inidents described in a Field Satety Notice □ Common and well documented incidents

evidence.claim: The Periodic Summary Report can be based on observed failure mode or root cause.
evidence.evidenceText: Stage of PSR reporting based on: □ Observed Failure mode □ Root cause

evidence.claim: The document contains a Trend Report form.
The Trend Report includes fields for date identified, trend narrative, analysis period, and trigger level.
evidence.evidenceText: Date the trend was identified; Description narrative for identified trend; Time period of trend analysis; Established trigger level

evidence.claim: The Trend Report asks whether trended events were submitted individually as reportable vigilance events.
evidence.evidenceText: Have any of the trended events been submitted individually as reportable events under vigilance? □ Yes □ No

evidence.claim: The forms include medical device classification options under AIMD, IVD, and MDD classes.
evidence.evidenceText: Class : □ AIMD Active implants □ IVD Annex II List A □ MDD Class III □ IVD Annex II List B □ MDD Class IIb □ IVD Devices for self-testing □ MDD Class IIa □ IVD General □ MDD Class I

evidence.claim: The forms prefer GMDN for nomenclature information.
The forms include distribution coverage for EEA, Switzerland, and Turkey.
evidence.evidenceText: The medical device has been distributed to the following Countries: Within EEA, Switzerland and Turkey

evidence.claim: The forms state that submission is not itself a conclusion that the report is complete or accurate or that the device failed or caused harm.
evidence.evidenceText: Submission of this report does not, in itself, represent a conclusion by the manufacturer and / or authorized representative or the National Competent Authority that the content of this report is complete or accurate, that the medical device(s) listed failed in any manner and/or that the medical device(s) caused or contributed to the alleged death or deterioration in the state of the health of any person.

evidence.claim: The form set identifies revisions APF808-1 through APF808-4 as REV.0.

reviewFlags.reason: No document date is present in the extracted form text.
No specific product model is filled in the source text; product-related metadata appears only in the source path or productSignal.
Authority is represented generically as National Competent Authority/NCA rather than a named national authority.
The source appears to be a blank form template set with OCR/spelling artifacts such as 'Indentify', 'Inaial', 'nember', and 'launguage'.
